title: 10 best herbal teas for swollen gums
intro_desc: Herbal teas for swollen gums are natural remedies that help alleviate the inflammation and discomfort associated with gum swelling, often caused by gum disease, dental procedures, or injury. The best herbal teas for swollen gums include Echinacea purpurea, Salvia officinalis, Eucalyptus globulus, Calendula officinalis, Ginkgo biloba, Achillea millefolium, Commiphora molmol, Aloe barbadensis, Glycyrrhiza glabra, and Sanguinaria canadensis. If left untreated, swollen gums can lead to painful chewing, bad breath, and difficulty speaking, affecting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that can help with swollen gums. This is because the plant has anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce swelling and pain. When we drink Echinacea purpurea tea, the active ingredients are absorbed into our body and work to calm down the inflammation in the gums. This can help to reduce the redness and swelling, making it easier to eat and speak. Echinacea purpurea tea also has antibacterial properties, which can help to fight off infections that may be causing the swelling. By reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, Echinacea purpurea tea can help to promote healing and prevent further problems with the gums. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea regularly may also help to strengthen the immune system, which can help to prevent recurring gum problems.

Salvia officinalis tea is good for swollen gums because it has strong anti-inflammatory properties. This means it can help reduce swelling and pain in the gums. The tea contains compounds like tannins and flavonoids, which are known to fight off infection and soothe irritated tissues. When you drink Salvia officinalis tea, these compounds go into your body and start working to calm down the swelling in your gums. This can help you feel more comfortable and ease the discomfort caused by swollen gums. Regular consumption of the tea may also help prevent future gum infections by keeping the bacteria that cause them under control. Additionally, Salvia officinalis tea has antimicrobial properties that can help kill the bacteria that cause gum inflammation, which can lead to conditions like gingivitis.

Eucalyptus globulus tea has been used for a long time to help swollen gums. This is because it has natural ingredients that reduce swelling and fight germs. The tea contains compounds that block the production of a chemical that causes swelling, making it easier to treat swollen gums. The antibacterial properties also help to kill the germs that cause the swelling in the first place. When we drink eucalyptus globulus tea, the compounds in the tea go into our body and help to calm down the swelling and kill the germs, making it a great natural remedy for swollen gums.  Eucalyptus globulus tea also contains antioxidants, which help to protect the gums from damage and promote healing. This makes it a great choice for people who want to avoid harsh chemicals and medications. The tea is also easy to make and can be drunk hot or cold, making it a convenient option for people who need relief from swollen gums.

Calendula officinalis tea is a natural remedy for swollen gums. The tea is made from the flowers of the Calendula plant, which have anti-inflammatory properties. When you drink the tea, the antioxidants in it help reduce swelling in the gums by fighting off the free radicals that cause inflammation. The anti-inflammatory properties also help ease pain and discomfort in the gums, making it easier to eat and speak. Additionally, Calendula tea has antimicrobial properties, which help prevent infections in the gums by killing bacteria and other microorganisms. This tea is also rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help protect the gums from damage and promote healing. By reducing swelling, easing pain, and preventing infections, Calendula officinalis tea can help soothe and calm swollen gums, allowing them to heal naturally.

Hydrastis canadensis tea, also known as goldenseal tea, is a natural remedy that can help with swollen gums. The active ingredient berberine in goldenseal tea has antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can reduce swelling and fight off infections in the gums. This can be especially helpful for people with gum disease or those who have experienced an injury to the gums. When you drink goldenseal tea, the berberine can reach the affected area and start working to reduce inflammation and kill bacteria. Additionally, goldenseal tea may also help to soothe and calm irritated gums, making it a popular choice for people who experience painful or sore gums. Drinking goldenseal tea regularly may also help to prevent future gum infections and promote overall gum health.

Ginkgo biloba tea is a natural remedy that can help with swollen gums. This is because it has anti-inflammatory properties, which reduce swelling and pain. The tea is made from the leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree, and it contains flavonoids and terpenoids, which are compounds that fight inflammation. When you drink Ginkgo biloba tea, these compounds go into your body and help to reduce the swelling in your gums. This can help to ease the pain and discomfort of swollen gums. The tea also has antiseptic properties, which can help to prevent infection and promote healing. This makes Ginkgo biloba tea a good choice for people who want to naturally treat swollen gums. The flavonoids in the tea also have antioxidant properties, which can help to protect your gums from damage caused by free radicals. This can help to keep your gums healthy and strong.

Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, is a good remedy for swollen gums. The main reason is that ginger has anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce swelling and pain. When we drink ginger tea, the active compounds in ginger go into our bloodstream and reach our gums. These compounds then reduce the inflammation and swelling, making our gums feel better. Ginger also has antimicrobial properties, which help fight off the bacteria that cause gum infections and gum disease. Additionally, ginger has a soothing effect, which can help calm down our gums and reduce the pain and discomfort. Drinking ginger tea regularly can also help prevent gum problems by keeping our gums healthy and strong.

Achillea millefolium tea, also known as yarrow tea, is a natural remedy that can help soothe swollen gums. This is because the tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenoids, which can reduce swelling and ease pain. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties can help calm the inflammation in the gums, making it a good option for people who experience gum inflammation due to dental problems, such as gingivitis or abscesses. Additionally, yarrow tea has antibacterial properties, which can help prevent the growth of bacteria that can cause gum infections. Drinking yarrow tea may also help promote healing and reduce bleeding in the gums, making it a potential natural remedy for people with gum problems. Yarrow tea is easy to make and can be consumed hot or cold, making it a convenient option for people who want to try a natural remedy for swollen gums.

Melaleuca alternifolia tea, also known as tea tree oil tea, is good for swollen gums because of its antibacterial properties. The tea tree oil in this tea helps kill the bacteria that cause gum infections and inflammation. When bacteria multiply in the gums, they can cause pain, redness, and swelling. Tea tree oil has been shown to reduce the growth of these bacteria, which in turn reduces swelling and pain in the gums. Additionally, tea tree oil has anti-inflammatory properties that help calm down the gums and reduce inflammation. This can help bring down the swelling and make it easier to manage gum infections.

Gaultheria procumbens tea is a natural remedy that may help soothe swollen gums. The tea is made from the leaves and stems of the Gaultheria procumbens plant, which is also known as wintergreen. The plant contains a chemical called methyl salicylate, which is similar to aspirin. When consumed as a tea, methyl salicylate may help reduce inflammation and pain in the gums. This can be especially helpful for people with gum disease or gingivitis, who often experience swollen, red, and painful gums. Drinking Gaultheria procumbens tea may also help kill bacteria that can cause gum infections. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce swelling and discomfort in the gums, allowing them to heal faster. Some people also use the tea to help manage mouth sores and other oral health issues.
